text.title: agentic-rag-iterative-retrieval Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agentic-rag-iterative-retrieval. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Iterative retrieval MUST cap the loop at max_iterations (recommended 3, hard cap 5). When the cap is reached without a sufficient verdict, log the failure and route to human review rather than continuing to burn budget.
rule.rationale: Unbounded loops diverge: the sufficiency check LLM may never return "sufficient", burning unlimited API budget. A hard cap keeps latency and cost predictable.

rule.statement: Use a cheaper model (Haiku or Sonnet) for the sufficiency check and query refinement steps. Reserve Opus for final answer synthesis.
rule.rationale: Sufficiency checks are yes/no judgements that do not require reasoning depth; routing them to Haiku reduces per-iteration cost by ~10x vs Opus.

rule.statement: Deduplicate context chunks between iterations using chunk IDs before passing context to the sufficiency judge or generator.
rule.rationale: The same chunk can surface in multiple retrieval passes; duplicating it inflates token usage and can bias the sufficiency judge.

rule.statement: Detect query drift: compute cosine similarity between the original query embedding and the refined query embedding. If similarity drops below 0.7, reset the refined query to the original.
rule.rationale: After 2+ iterations the refinement can drift from the original intent — the refined query answers a different question than the one the user asked.
